title: Recommendation Algorithm Intern - Short Video
text: The role focuses on recall and ranking for short video feeds. Candidates should understand two-tower retrieval, negative sampling, time-based split, Recall@K, NDCG@K, and leakage checks for impression-level logs.

title: Search and Retrieval Algorithm Intern
text: The team works on hybrid retrieval, semantic matching, query rewrite, and reranking. Offline evaluation uses Recall@5, MRR, citation hit rate, and manual badcase review before any answer generation is added.

title: Short Video Recommendation Reproduction
text: The baseline used a two-tower model with user sequence features and item category embeddings. Time decay, user-category crosses, author interaction features, and sampled negatives improved Recall@50 from 0.112 to 0.126 on the validation day.

title: Ecommerce Multimodal Retrieval
text: Hard negatives were built from same-category products with similar titles or images but conflicting SKU attributes. The review table grouped false positives into SKU conflict, marketing OCR noise, title generalization, and same-category different-style cases.

title: RAG Interview Follow-up Questions
text: For RAG, Recall@K checks whether evidence is retrieved, MRR rewards the rank of the first correct evidence, citation hit rate checks whether generated answers cite retrieved evidence, and unsupported-answer rate tracks hallucination risk.

title: Job Knowledge Base RAG Evaluation
text: Chunk size 500 with overlap 100 was selected because it preserved complete job requirement context while avoiding long chunks that diluted retrieval targets. Chunk size 300 increased fragment noise and chunk size 800 reduced query-document specificity.

title: Job Knowledge Base RAG Evaluation
text: The final retrieval pipeline used BM25 first-stage retrieval, dense embedding retrieval with Faiss FlatIP-style cosine search, RRF fusion, and a lightweight reranker. The strongest run improved Recall@5 from 0.71 to 0.77.

title: Recommendation Whiteboard Questions
text: In recommendation recall, exposure without click can be treated as a weak negative, but sampling must avoid future information and popularity leakage. Time split is safer than random split for validating ranking and recall experiments.
